summary: A stellar late‑'82 Cap Centre performance captured on a half‑audience/half‑soundboard matrix with crisp sound after an early rough patch, highlighted by the Playin > Crazy Fingers combo and the first ever Touch of Grey.
review: The show is consistently praised for its high energy first set, superb Playin > Crazy Fingers sequence, and historic Touch of Grey encore, with many reviewers calling it a must‑have. The recording is a matrix (SBD‑AUD) that sounds crisp once the mix settles about halfway through, though the opening moments are a bit muddy. Overall the musical performance outweighs any minor technical hiccups.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (12/14) give 4–5 stars and rave about the performance; recording is generally good with only early‑set issues, yielding a weighted rating around 4.4.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crisp and great quality after the first few minutes; early portion noted as rough/muddy but improves dramatically around the Crazy Fingers segment. Half audience / half soundboard mix.

standout_songs: Touch of Grey (first ever live performance)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers highlight a unique set‑list with the Playin/Crazy Fingers/Playing Jam sequence weaving through both sets, a high‑energy first set and a solid second set featuring strong solos and vocal improv.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praise his “noodles” on Let It Grow and Shakedown, calling them excellent.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work is lauded as hitting his stride, though one reviewer criticizes his tone on Shakedown.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drums segment noted as “sick”; no further specific comments.
